title: sempervivum ciliosum subsp. micevskii
intro: Sempervivum ciliosum subsp. micevskii, also known as Mouse's Tail, is a succulent plant species of the genus Sempervivum, characterized by its rosettes of fleshy, silvery-gray leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Sempervivum ciliosum subsp. micevskii include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support wound healing, making it a valuable addition to topical applications and herbal remedies. However, I couldn't find any information on specific herbal preparations or commercial products featuring this plant.
